headline: What if editing video required zero clicks?
No timeline. No keyframes. No license.
body: A video editing agent on GitHub now handles trimming, color grading, speed changes, and transitions through plain English instructions. No timeline. No keyframes. No Premiere Pro license.

headline: Natural language becomes the editing tool
body: The Video Use Skill from awesome-genmedia processes natural language commands like "make the first 10 seconds faster" or "add a warm color grade to the interview segments" and executes them deterministically.
image_keyword: natural language commands text to video

headline: 70-80% of editing in under 3 minutes
body: Professional editors charge $75-200/hour. A single 5-minute corporate video typically costs $2,500-8,000 to produce. AI agents handle 70-80% of the mechanical editing work in under 3 minutes.

headline: Creative decisions stay human
body: This is not replacing creative editors. It is replacing the 4 hours spent syncing audio, adjusting white balance, and cutting dead air. The creative decisions still belong to humans. The grunt work is gone.

headline: 14x video growth. Zero bottleneck.
body: Video content volume is projected to grow 14x by 2027. The bottleneck was never ideas. It was editing labor. AI agents just removed that bottleneck entirely.

headline: Follow for more on AI media tools
